evidence: The paper uses random forests with **permutation importance** (not impurity-based) in Studies 1-2 to identify /r/ as the "most strongly predictive phoneme." However, the main conclusions depend on Bayesian logistic regression models and effect sizes: "63% of rough words contain an /r/, with a 95% credible interval of [51%, 75%]" vs. 30% of smooth words. Studies 3-4 abandon random forests entirely, using only Bayesian regression. The random forest ranking is exploratory; hypothesis tests and cross-linguistic replication provide the substantive evidence.
